microsoft.iis.virtual_directory.short_description: Configures a virtual directory in IIS
microsoft.iis.virtual_directory.description: Creates, removes, and configures a virtual directory in IIS.

application.description: The application under which the virtual directory is created or exists.

connect_as.description: The type of authentication to use for the virtual directory. Either V(pass_through) or V(specific_user). If V(pass_through), IIS uses the identity of the user or application pool identity to access the physical path. If V(specific_user), IIS uses the credentials provided in username and password to access the physical path.

name.description: The name of the virtual directory to create.

password.description: The password associated with O(username). Required when O(connect_as=specific_user).

physical_path.description: The physical path to the folder in which the new virtual directory is created. The specified folder must already exist. This must be set when O(state=present) and the virtual directory does not already exist.

site.description: The site name under which the virtual directory should exist.

state.description: Whether to add or remove the specified virtual directory. Removing will delete the virtual directory and all its contents recursively.

username.description: Specifies the username of an account that can access configuration files and content for the virtual directory. Required when O(connect_as=specific_user).

microsoft.iis.web_app_pool.description: Creates, removes and configures an IIS Web Application Pool.

attributes.description: This field is a free form dictionary value for the application pool attributes. These attributes are based on the naming standard at U(https://www.iis.net/configreference/system.applicationhost/applicationpools/add#005), see the examples section for more details on how to set this. You can also set the attributes of child elements like cpu and processModel, see the examples to see how it is done. While you can use the numeric values for enums it is recommended to use the enum name itself, e.g., use SpecificUser instead of 3 for processModel.identityType. managedPipelineMode may be either "Integrated" or "Classic". startMode may be either "OnDemand" or "AlwaysRunning". Use O(state) to modify the state of the app pool. When trying to set C(processModel.password) and you receive a 'Value does fall within the expected range' error, you have a corrupted keystore. Please follow U(http://structuredsight.com/2014/10/26/im-out-of-range-youre-out-of-range/) to help fix your host.

state.description: The state of the application pool. If V(absent) will ensure the app pool is removed. If V(present) will ensure the app pool is configured and exists. If V(restarted) will ensure the app pool exists and will restart, this is never idempotent. If V(started) will ensure the app pool exists and is started. If V(stopped) will ensure the app pool exists and is stopped.

microsoft.iis.web_application.description: Creates, removes, and configures IIS web applications.

application_pool.description: The application pool in which the new site executes. If not specified, the application pool of the current website will be used.

connect_as.description: The type of authentication to use for this application. Either V(pass_through) or V(specific_user). If V(pass_through), IIS will use the identity of the user or application pool identity to access the file system or network. If V(specific_user), IIS will use the credentials provided in O(username) and O(password) to access the file system or network. If not specified, the default is V(pass_through) and an existing application will not be modified.

password.description: The password associated with O(username). Required when O(connect_as=specific_user).

physical_path.description: The physical path on the remote host to use for the new application. The specified folder must already exist.

site.description: Name of the site on which the application is created.

state.description: State of the web application. If V(absent), the web application will be removed. If V(present), the web application will be created if not already present.

username.description: Specifies the user name of an account that can access configuration files and content for this application. Required when O(connect_as=specific_user).

microsoft.iis.website.description: Creates, removes, and configures an IIS website.

application_pool.description: Specifies the application pool in which the website runs. The application pool must already exist - otherwise the website will not start.

bindings.description: Specifies the website bindings to add, remove or set. The combination of C(ip:port:hostname) is the binding unique identifier. The binding C(ip:port) must be available and not used by any other website on the server. To clear all website bindings, use O(bindings.set) with an empty list.

name.description: The name of the website to manage.

physical_path.description: Specifies the physical folder path on the remote host to use for the website. When create a new website physical_path is required. The specified folder must already exist.

preload_enabled.description: Specifies whether application preloading is enabled for the website. When V(true), IIS will automatically initialize the application when the application pool starts or when a configuration change is detected. This is useful for reducing the initial response time for the first request to the application. For this to work effectively, the associated application pool (see M(microsoft.iis.web_app_pool)) should have C(startMode=AlwaysRunning).

site_id.description: Explicitly set the IIS website numeric ID. Note that this value cannot be changed after the website has been created. The site ID must be available and not used by any other website on the server.

state.description: State of the website. In order to start the website at least one binding should be configured.

name.description: Name of the website. When not specified, information of all existing websites will be fetched.
